title: Polymath project context
description: Project-level localization for `.polymath/project.yaml`. Loaded by the polymath-core SessionStart hook and exposed to all skills as a resolved JSON snapshot under `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA}/polymath-core/project-context.json`. Lets a project tell Polymath what stack it uses (language, framework, testing tools), what conventions it follows (commit style, code-review checklist), which external skill catalogs to surface, and how to override specific skill behavior (additional review axes, test commands). Capability resolution is owned by `.polymath/capabilities.yaml` (separate file, separate schema); this file may reference it via `capabilities.inherit_from` but does not duplicate the data. The schema is intentionally minimal — start with the fields here, extend conservatively as patterns prove out.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version. v1 is the only supported version.

project.description: Identification + a one-line description surfaced in the SessionStart welcome.

stack.description: What this project is built on. Skills that adapt to language / framework read these fields.

conventions.description: House rules: commit style, code-review checklist, branch strategy, language-specific style files.

external_skills.description: External skill catalogs (e.g. dotnet/skills, laravel/boost) that this project recommends. SessionStart surfaces these so contributors discover them; install is out-of-band (catalog-specific).

capabilities.description: Pointer to `.polymath/capabilities.yaml` so capability resolution can find a non-default location. The actual capability data lives in that file; not duplicated here.

setup.description: Onboarding and local setup metadata inferred from README, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs, CI, and project config files. Used to tell coding agents which context to read, which tools to check, which environment variables matter, and what first steps a new contributor should take.

context_sources.description: Repo files an agent should read first for project-specific expectations.

required_tools.description: Tools expected in the local environment.

environment.description: Environment variables, API keys, and tokens a contributor may need. Sensitive values are named only; never store values here.

first_steps.description: Concrete first actions after installing the recommended plugins.

polymath.description: Polymath-specific activation hints: recommended plugins, workflows, and compatible agent surfaces for this repository.

compatible_agents.description: Agent or IDE surfaces the project explicitly supports. Capabilities vary by surface; see docs/PORTABILITY.md.

skill_overrides.description: Per-skill customization. Key is `<plugin>:<skill>`. Each entry may declare additional context files, additional review axes, or test commands the skill should run.

prompts.description: Pointers to project-owned artifact templates that skills should prefer over their built-in templates. One optional key per artifact type in the catalog's artifact vocabulary.

conventions_docs.description: Convention documents by role. Key is a doc-type role (e.g. `backend-stack`, `deployment`, `review-checklist`, `knowledge-base`); value is a repo-relative path. Skills that adapt to project conventions resolve documents by role here instead of hardcoding filenames, so any project can supply its own corpus.

smoke.description: Boot-verification recipes per language: how to start the app and confirm it actually serves. Tests passing does not imply the app runs; verification gates consume these recipes to close that gap. Key matches `stack.languages[].lang`.

readiness.description: Health endpoint path/URL or boot-log pattern that signals the app is up.

credentials_file.description: Gitignored machine-local env file needed to boot-verify auth-gated apps. Path only — the file itself is never committed and its values are never logged.

tracker.description: Work-item destination and provenance marking for tracker integrations. The provider is resolved via `.polymath/capabilities.yaml` (issue_tracker); this block only says where agent-created items land and how they are marked. Secrets never live here.

work_item_types.description: Map from Polymath item categories (e.g. `bug`, `backlog`, `feedback-pattern`) to the tracker's work-item type names.

marking.description: Provenance marking applied to every agent-created item: title prefix for board visibility, tag for one-query filtering. A traceability footer is generated by the creating skill.

routing.description: How Polymath engages with prompts in this project. `hint` (default) is the propose-first routing hint shipping today; `classify` and `enforce` activate the opt-in polymath-pipeline plugin — a per-prompt classify directive, and (enforce) a gate that blocks mutating tool calls until the request is classified. Without that plugin installed the values are inert.

attribution.description: Output attribution preferences: whether plugin components prefix chat-visible output with origin markers, and an optional Co-Authored-By trailer for agent-assisted commits (absent = no trailer).

artifact_matrix.description: Repo-relative path to the project's artifact matrix: which artifacts (PRD/ADR/DRD/...) are required per work type and scope. Workflow guards consume it as an advisory check.
